name: Nightshade Cockpit
vibe: A near-black RGB-peripheral cockpit ruled by one vivid cyan accent (#21D4D9) with violet (#926DDE) as a quiet secondary for modifier/layer keys. The chassis reads like milled dark aluminum under a soft top light: keycaps have genuine 3D convexity from top-biased vertical gradients plus a single coherent micro-shadow plane, panels are restrained dark slabs over a subtle top-spotlight vignette, and the one selected key ignites with a cyan halo so it always wins the eye. Type and spacing carry hierarchy; monospaced tabular numerics give an instrument-readout feel. Tight, technical, confident, dark and precise — Wooting-grade restraint, not Razer circus. The 'single glow law' (only ONE element glows at full strength at a time — the selected key, or the slider being dragged) is the soul of the design and the thing that makes it feel like a real RGB suite rather than a default-SwiftUI hack job.
appearance: dark — locked, not adaptive. Force it: set `.preferredColorScheme(.dark)` on the root view AND in Info-less swiftc builds also pin `NSApp.appearance = NSAppearance(named: .darkAqua)` at launch (e.g. in the App init or an AppDelegate adaptor) so native menus, the menu-bar battery item, popovers, and any stray AppKit chrome match. All custom surfaces use explicit hex (no semantic system colors) so they never drift with system settings. Do NOT support light mode — the entire convex-keycap + cyan-glow language depends on a near-black canvas; a light variant would dilute the concept. The one concession to system: honor Reduce Motion by collapsing the keycap/slider/glow animations to instant (gate every withAnimation on `accessibilityReduceMotion`).
typography: One family — SF Pro (system .default design); NO serif, NO rounded anywhere (rounded would soften the technical instrument vibe). Weight contrast carries hierarchy; never use .bold for body. Roles: TITLE (header section title, e.g. "Keymap"): .system(size:17, weight:.semibold), tracking +0.3, color textPrimary #E8EAF0. HEADER SUBTITLE (contextual line under the title, e.g. "Layer 0 · Base" / effect name / "3 macros"): .system(size:12, weight:.regular), color textSecondary #8A90A0, monospacedDigit when it shows a count. SECTION LABEL (sidebar nav, slider captions, panel headers): .system(size:11, weight:.semibold), .textCase(.uppercase), kerning 1.4, color textSecondary #8A90A0. KEY LEGEND: single-char letters .system(size:12, weight:.medium); multi-char legends (esc, ctrl, shift, enter) .system(size:9.5, weight:.medium) with .lineLimit(1).minimumScaleFactor(0.8) — color #C7CBD6 at rest, #E8EAF0 on hover, #A6F4F7 when selected. NUMERIC READOUTS / CAPTIONS (slider values, H/S/V, battery %, macro indices): .system(size:12, weight:.medium, design:.monospaced) with .monospacedDigit() so values like 78% and H 184 don't jitter — color accent #21D4D9 for live/changing values, textSecondary #8A90A0 for static units.
keycap: Build ONE reusable `KeycapView: View, Equatable` driven by an enum `KeyVisualState { case rest, hover, selected, modifier }` (selected wins over modifier). Equatable key = (keycode, state) so unchanged caps skip re-render when one key flips. SHAPE: RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius:9, style:.continuous). SIZE: width = w*UNIT - GUTTER, height = h*UNIT - GUTTER, UNIT=46, GUTTER=5 (a 1u cap ≈ 41pt). Precompute all four fill gradients + the border gradient as `static let` so 82 instances share them. REST FILL: LinearGradient(top→bottom) stops [#2A2D38 @0.0, #191B22 @0.52, #101218 @1.0] — top-lit, reads convex. BORDER: .overlay(shape.strokeBorder(LinearGradient(top white.opacity(0.16) → bottom white.opacity(0.08)), lineWidth:0.5)) — brighter top edge fakes a lit bevel. TOP INNER HIGHLIGHT: overlay a 1pt-tall RoundedRectangle pinned to the top inset with white.opacity(0.05). DEPTH: exactly ONE shadow — .shadow(color:.black.opacity(0.55), radius:5, x:0, y:3). LEGEND: centered Text per typography (single-char vs multi-char). MODIFIER (Fn/layer/modifier caps): fill [#23202B @0, #171520 @0.52, #0F0E16 @1], border in accent2 #926DDE (top 0.5 → bottom 0.4 alpha), legend #C7CBD6 — distinguishes layer keys by tint, no glow. HOVER: lighten fill one notch [#333744,#22242E,#181A21], border alpha up (top 0.24/bottom 0.14), shadow radius 9 y4, legend #E8EAF0; animate .easeOut(0.12). SELECTED (the HERO, only one at a time): fill cyan-tinted [#1B3A44 @0, #13313C @0.52, #0E2630 @1], border solid accent #21D4D9 lineWidth 1, legend #A6F4F7 weight .semibold, top inner highlight rgba(160,245,248,0.25), and TWO stacked glows ONLY on this cap: .shadow(color:accent.opacity(0.55), radius:14) + .shadow(color:accent.opacity(0.85), radius:4). Use SF Symbols for icon-only caps (delete.left, arrow.up/down/left/right, return, capslock) at 12–13pt in the legend color; everything else plain Text.
sidebar: Fixed 180pt rail. Background: Color(hex:"#14161D").opacity(0.70) layered OVER .ultraThinMaterial (material behind the tint, NOT a manual .blur) for a glassy frosted slab over the near-black window; right edge a 0.5pt divider in border #23262F. Top: 26×26 app glyph = RoundedRectangle(cornerRadius:8, style:.continuous) filled LinearGradient(135°, #21D4D9 → #0E8F93) with a keyboard SF Symbol in #0A0B0F, plus .shadow(color:accent.opacity(0.4), radius:8, y:2), beside a 15pt semibold "KeebTool" wordmark in textPrimary. NAV ROWS (Keymap/Macros/Lighting): 36pt tall, 8pt continuous corner, leading SF Symbol (keyboard / command / lightbulb) at 13pt + SECTION LABEL text. INACTIVE: clear fill, icon+text textSecondary #8A90A0; hover fill surfaceAlt #1A1C24, text → #C7CBD6. ACTIVE: fill LinearGradient(135°, #21D4D9 → #0E8F93), icon+text #0A0B0F weight .semibold, .shadow(color:accent.opacity(0.4), radius:10) so the live tab glows like a lit key (this is allowed alongside the selected keycap — they're in different panes). DEVICE-STATUS BUTTON pinned bottom via Spacer: bordered pill (RoundedRectangle radius 8 continuous, surfaceAlt #1A1C24 fill, 0.5pt border #23262F) with a 7pt status dot (#5CE0A0 + soft 6pt green glow, gently pulsing scale+opacity repeatForever when connected; #6B7180 dim, no glow, no pulse when offline) and two stacked lines "V1 Max" 11pt medium textPrimary / "Connected · 82%" 11pt monospacedDigit textSecondary. Whole pill is tappable to open the connection sheet.
header: ~56pt bar, NO opaque fill — sits directly on the pane's radial top-spotlight background, separated by a 0.5pt bottom hairline #23262F. LEFT: a 3pt-wide × 16pt-tall accent cyan capsule (RoundedRectangle radius 1.5, fill #21D4D9) as a 'you are here' marker, then a VStack(alignment:.leading, spacing:1) with the section TITLE (17/semibold textPrimary) and the contextual SUBTITLE (12/regular textSecondary — Keymap→"Layer 0 · Base", Lighting→live effect name, Macros→"N macros"). RIGHT: HStack of action buttons, 8pt spacing (see controls — secondary like Reset, primary like Save/Apply). PANE BACKGROUND: ZStack base Color(hex:"#0A0B0F") with a RadialGradient(center:.top, startRadius:0, endRadius:~520, colors:[#14161D @0, #0A0B0F @0.7]) overlaid — a subtle spotlight-from-above so center keycaps catch slightly more light than the edges, reinforcing the top-lit convexity.
controls: BUTTONS: 30pt tall, 8pt continuous radius, .system(13,.medium), .buttonStyle(.plain) with custom states. SECONDARY (Reset/default): fill surfaceAlt #1A1C24, 0.5pt border #23262F, text #C7CBD6; hover fill #22242E. PRIMARY (Save/Apply): fill LinearGradient(135°, #21D4D9 → #0E8F93), text #0A0B0F semibold, .shadow(color:accent.opacity(0.4), radius:10); pressed .scaleEffect(0.97). SLIDERS (custom — replace stock NSSlider via DragGesture over a GeometryReader; clamp, format readout with String(format:) + .monospacedDigit()): 5pt track RoundedRectangle(radius 2.5) fill #22242E; filled portion LinearGradient(90°, #0E8F93 → #21D4D9). Thumb = 15pt Circle fill #E8EAF0 with a thin 0.5pt dark ring (#0A0B0F). GLOW LAW: the filled track + thumb gain .shadow(color:accent.opacity(0.6/0.9), radius:8) ONLY while that slider is actively being dragged (isDragging) — idle sliders are flat, so the dragged slider is the one live glow. Readout sits right-aligned in fixed width, monospaced accent. HUE slider track = full-spectrum LinearGradient(red→…→red); SATURATION track = gray→current-hue gradient (these teach the color space). EFFECT DROPDOWN: Menu styled as a surfaceAlt #1A1C24 pill, 8pt radius, 0.5pt border #23262F, label 13pt #C7CBD6 + trailing chevron.down 11pt textSecondary. COLOR PREVIEW: 34pt RoundedRectangle(radius 8, continuous) filled with the live HSV color, 0.5pt border white.opacity(0.1), and .shadow(color: thatColor.opacity(0.5), radius:12) so it glows its own hue (one shadow on one view — cheap, on-metaphor). KEYCODE PICKER: a .popover (lazy, built only on key tap) — a frosted glass panel (Color #14161D.opacity(0.7) over .ultraThinMaterial, 0.5pt border #23262F, radius 12) with a searchable LazyVGrid of small keycap-styled buttons reusing KeycapView's rest/hover look; selecting writes the keycode and dismisses. MACRO LIST: 8pt-spaced rows (NOT stock List/Form), each a surfaceAlt #1A1C24 capsule (radius 8, 0.5pt border #23262F) with a leading monospaced index, a TextField(.plain) 13pt monospaced (placeholder textSecondary), and a trailing trash SF Symbol button (textSecondary → #E24B4A on hover); focused row gets a 0.5pt accent border + faint accent glow (the focused-field == the one live glow in that pane).
signatureDetails: The single-glow law: at most ONE element glows at full strength at any moment — the selected keycap, the slider being dragged, the focused macro field, or the active nav tab in the rail. Everything else stays restrained, so the glow always reads as genuinely 'live' rather than decorative. This one rule is what separates it from cheap configurators that glow everything.

Top-lit convexity: every keycap's brighter top border edge + top-biased 3-stop gradient + a 1pt white top-inner highlight makes the board look physically lit from above — and the pane's RadialGradient top spotlight makes center caps catch more light than edges, so the lighting is coherent across the whole grid, not per-cap.

One coherent micro-shadow plane: every rest/hover cap casts exactly ONE drop shadow with identical params, so 82 caps read as a single machined grid with one shadow plane — instead of 82 fuzzy competing halos, which is the #1 tell of a hack-job configurator.

Frosted-glass chrome via .ultraThinMaterial under a tinted color (sidebar + keycode popover) lets the near-black bg bleed through, giving real depth layering like an actual RGB cockpit — never a manual .blur (too expensive).
Monospaced tabular numerics everywhere a value changes (slider %, H/S/V, battery, macro indices) for an instrument-readout feel; live values in cyan, static units in gray.
Violet (#926DDE) reserved exclusively as a modifier/layer-key tint — present enough to organize the keymap at a glance, never loud enough to compete with the cyan selection glow.
Objects that emit color cast their own colored shadow: the color preview swatch glows its live HSV hue, the primary Save button and active nav tab cast cyan — selling 'light is emitted, not painted'.
The header's 3pt cyan 'you are here' capsule + contextual subtitle gives each pane a precise sense of place without chrome noise.
A softly pulsing green connected-dot (#5CE0A0 with halo) at the bottom of the rail — a tiny living 'device is alive' heartbeat.
swiftuiNotes: BACKGROUND: root window is Color(hex:"#0A0B0F"); each detail pane overlays a RadialGradient(center:.top, [#14161D @0 → #0A0B0F @0.7]) for the top spotlight. The sidebar/keycode popover use .background(.ultraThinMaterial) with a Color(hex:"#14161D").opacity(0.70) tint layered over it — NEVER a manual .blur (offscreen-pass cost). APPEARANCE: lock dark — `.preferredColorScheme(.dark)` on root plus `NSApp.appearance = NSAppearance(named:.darkAqua)` at launch so native menus / menu-bar battery item / popovers match; use only explicit hex (no semantic colors) so nothing drifts. REUSABLE COMPONENTS TO CREATE: (1) `enum Theme` (or a `Palette` caseless enum) holding every hex as `static let` Color values via a Color(hex:) initializer — single source of truth, no asset catalog. (2) `Color(hex:)` init helper (there's no asset catalog, so hardcode all colors through it). (3) `KeycapView: View, Equatable` taking a Key model + KeyVisualState; its four fill gradients + border gradient are `static let`. (4) A `.cardBackground()` ViewModifier = RoundedRectangle(radius 8, continuous).fill(surfaceAlt) + 0.5pt border #23262F, reused by buttons/macro rows/status pill. (5) A `.frostedPanel()` modifier = tinted color over .ultraThinMaterial + hairline border, reused by sidebar + popover. (6) A custom `KeebSlider` (DragGesture over GeometryReader, isDragging-gated glow, monospaced readout). (7) A `PrimaryButtonStyle` / `SecondaryButtonStyle` pair. LAYOUT: place keys with absolute .position(x:y:) computed from x/y/w/h*UNIT inside a fixed-size ZStack (wrap in a GeometryReader only to center/scale the canvas) — NOT nested HStacks/Spacers; cleaner for the irregular 82-key grid and avoids layout thrash. PERFORMANCE (82 caps): CRITICAL — apply the two glow .shadow modifiers ONLY to the selected cap (1 of 82); rest/hover caps get a single cheap drop shadow. Never map a colored glow over all 82 (compositing 82 blurred shadows tanks fps). Drive selection through one `@Published selectedKeyID: Int?` on an ObservableObject — flipping it invalidates exactly two caps (old + new), not all 82. Make KeycapView Equatable on (keycode, state) and rely on that to skip re-render rather than .drawingGroup(); if you still measure jank, you MAY .drawingGroup() the NON-selected caps only (rasterize the static grid) while keeping the selected cap outside so its glow can animate — but do NOT .drawingGroup() over the material sidebar (drawingGroup fights vibrancy). Use .continuous corner style everywhere for the squircle/milled-edge look. Animate state with .easeOut(0.12) for hover, a soft .easeInOut for the selection 'ignite'; gate ALL withAnimation on accessibilityReduceMotion. Sliders: update the visual binding immediately but debounce the device-write side effect. AVOID: per-cap .blur, AngularGradient on caps (only the hue slider needs a spectrum gradient — use LinearGradient), any .shadow radius>~14 except the single selected glow, recreating Gradient objects in body (always static let), stock List/Form/Slider (build custom for the look + control), and wrapping the whole board in .drawingGroup if the selected glow must animate.
